pi_items.source: Cross-referenced with task description, export_result.sh (OUTPUT_PATH variable), and setup_task.sh (rm -f cleanup). All three reference the same path consistently.
Task description says '30 feet wide'. Room coordinates span X=0 to X=30 (living 0-18 + kitchen 18-30 = 30; bedroom1 0-12 + bathroom 12-18 + bedroom2 18-30 = 30). Rooms tile the full width perfectly.
Task description says '22 feet tall'. Room coordinates span Y=0 to Y=22 (bottom row 0-10, top row 10-22). Total room area 660 sq ft = 30 x 22 exactly.
Description says 'Living Room (18 x 12): top-left, from (0,10) to (18,22)'. Computed: 18-0=18' wide, 22-10=12' tall. Matches description exactly.
Description says 'Kitchen (12 x 12): top-right, from (18,10) to (30,22)'. Computed: 30-18=12' wide, 22-10=12' tall. Matches.
Description says 'Bedroom 1 (12 x 10): bottom-left, from (0,0) to (12,10)'. Computed: 12-0=12', 10-0=10'. Matches.
Description says 'Bathroom (6 x 10): bottom-center, from (12,0) to (18,10)'. Computed: 18-12=6', 10-0=10'. Matches.
Description says 'Bedroom 2 (12 x 10): bottom-right, from (18,0) to (30,10)'. Computed: 30-18=12', 10-0=10'. Matches.
Y=10 is the boundary between top row rooms (y_min=10) and bottom row rooms (y_max=10). Spans full width 0-30. Verified against room coordinates.
X=12 separates bedroom1 (x_max=12) from bathroom (x_min=12). Only runs in bottom section Y=0 to Y=10. Verified against room coordinates.
X=18 separates living (x_max=18) from kitchen (x_min=18) in top row, AND bathroom (x_max=18) from bedroom2 (x_min=18) in bottom row. Runs full height Y=0 to Y=22.
Center=9, width=3.0: gap = 9-1.5=7.5 to 9+1.5=10.5. North wall is at Y=22 (exterior_height). Calculation verified independently.
Center=16, width=3.0: gap = 16-1.5=14.5 to 16+1.5=17.5. On X=18 wall (interior wall between living and kitchen). Y=16 is within living/kitchen row (Y=10-22).
Center=6, width=3.0: gap = 6-1.5=4.5 to 6+1.5=7.5. On Y=10 wall. X=6 is within bedroom1 (X=0-12).
Center=24, width=3.0: gap = 24-1.5=22.5 to 24+1.5=25.5. On Y=10 wall. X=24 is within bedroom2 (X=18-30).
Center=16, width=5.0: gap = 16-2.5=13.5 to 16+2.5=18.5. West wall is at X=0. Y=16 is within living room (Y=10-22). Gap 13.5-18.5 is within living room Y range.
Center=16, width=4.0: gap = 16-2.0=14.0 to 16+2.0=18.0. East wall at X=30. Y=16 is within kitchen (Y=10-22). Gap within kitchen Y range.
Center=6, width=4.0: gap = 6-2.0=4.0 to 6+2.0=8.0. South wall at Y=0. X=6 is within bedroom1 (X=0-12). Gap within BR1 X range.
Center=5, width=4.0: gap = 5-2.0=3.0 to 5+2.0=7.0. East wall at X=30. Y=5 is within bedroom2 (Y=0-10). Gap within BR2 Y range.

AutoCAD Color Index (ACI) standard: 1=Red, 2=Yellow, 3=Green, 4=Cyan, 6=Magenta, 7=White. These are the standard ACI values used by LibreCAD. Cross-referenced with task description which names these exact colors for each layer.

1.5 feet tolerance on a 30x22 foot drawing is ~6.8% of the smaller dimension. Reasonable for accommodating minor coordinate imprecisions in manual CAD drawing. Used only for walls bounding box check in verifier.

Independently counted: 17 wall segments needed (North:2, East:3, South:2, West:2, Y=10:4, X=12:1, X=18:3). min_wall_segments=12 is 70.6% of 17, providing partial credit for incomplete drawings. Reasonable threshold.
5 doors total. min_door_arcs=3 is 60%, providing partial credit. Reasonable threshold for a task where arc drawing is known to be challenging for AI agents.
Task description asks for 'at least 10 linear dimensions'. min_dimensions=8 is the partial credit threshold in the verifier (awards 8/12 pts). Full marks require >=10. Reasonable.

Verifier checks for 4 keywords: LIVING, KITCHEN, BEDROOM, BATH. 'BEDROOM' matches both 'BEDROOM 1' and 'BEDROOM 2', and 'BATH' matches 'BATHROOM'. So 4 keywords effectively cover all 5 rooms. Requiring 4/4 means all keywords must appear.

60/100 pass threshold. An agent that gets layers correct (18pts) + partial walls (5-10pts) + some door arcs (4-8pts) + some fixtures (5pts) + partial labels (3-7pts) + footprint (5pts) could pass with 40-53pts. A complete attempt needs dimensions or text to pass. Consistent with existing LibreCAD very_hard tasks which use 60-65 thresholds.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic 2-bedroom apartment floor plan task. The apartment is 30 feet wide by 22 feet tall with origin at southwest corner (0,0). Five rooms in a grid: Living Room 18x12 at (0,10)-(18,22), Kitchen 12x12 at (18,10)-(30,22), Bedroom 1 12x10 at (0,0)-(12,10), Bathroom 6x10 at (12,0)-(18,10), Bedroom 2 12x10 at (18,0)-(30,10). Three interior walls: horizontal at Y=10 full width, vertical at X=12 from Y=0-10, vertical at X=18 from Y=0-22. Five doors with 90-degree arc swings: D1 entry on north wall at X=9 (3ft, gap 7.5-10.5), D2 living-kitchen on X=18 wall at Y=16 (3ft, gap 14.5-17.5), D3 living-BR1 on Y=10 wall at X=6 (3ft, gap 4.5-7.5), D4 living-bath on Y=10 wall at X=15 (2ft8in, gap 13.667-16.333), D5 living-BR2 on Y=10 wall at X=24 (3ft, gap 22.5-25.5). Four windows as parallel line pairs: W1 west wall at Y=16 (5ft, gap 13.5-18.5), W2 east wall at Y=16 (4ft, gap 14.0-18.0), W3 south wall at X=6 (4ft, gap 4.0-8.0), W4 east wall at Y=5 (4ft, gap 3.0-7.0). Walls drawn as single lines on WALLS layer (white/7), leaving gaps for all openings. Total of 17 wall line segments expected. Six required layers: WALLS (white/7), DOORS (red/1), WINDOWS (cyan/4), FIXTURES (green/3), DIMENSIONS (yellow/2), TEXT (magenta/6). Kitchen needs counter, sink circle, stove rectangle on FIXTURES layer. Bathroom needs bathtub, toilet, sink on FIXTURES layer. At least 10 dimension entities required. Room labels must contain keywords LIVING, KITCHEN, BEDROOM, BATH. Output saved as /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/apartment_plan.dxf.
